description:
EPIC: make the compiler2 semantic fixpoint converge by construction, and remove numeric literal types from the lattice (Elixir alignment). Design doc: eli5.html at the repo root — read it first; it explains the bug, the architecture, and the vocabulary (claims, ascents, ground shifts, rebase, honest paths, widening) used by every child ticket.

== THE BUG (verified from telemetry logs; details below) ==
./target/release/fz2 build on fixtures/quicksort/input.fz is bimodal: ~332 jobs, or 54,000+ jobs and a 700MB --log-telemetry file, decided by per-process HashSet wake ordering (deps.rs subscribers/waiters and follow_up sets iterate in RandomState order; the FIFO agenda then phase-locks whichever interleaving occurred). The runaway is a period-2 oscillation of AnalyzeActivation(qsort[list(int)]):

Phase A: the analyzer reads its own previous ReturnType = empty-list. qsort(lo) is then empty-list, so the append callsite demands the activation append[empty-list-key], which exists and returns list(int). qsort publishes ReturnType = list(int) (changed), which wakes its own subscribers — including itself (self-recursive functions read their own ReturnType fact).
Phase B: reading list(int), the append call now demands append[list-key] — different canonical key. Publishing the new contribution set drops the old one; the dropped activation facts retract (sole publisher); the retracted callee re-runs, finds its ActivationInputs fact gone, returns a wait with EMPTY outputs — and facts.rs replace_outputs treats unlisted outputs as retraction, so the callee retracts its own ReturnType. qsort then reads the absent return as the TYPE none (prepare_function_call in jobs/semantic.rs does activation_return(..).unwrap_or(none)), concludes ReturnType = empty-list (a DESCENT — define_return is last-write-wins), and the cycle repeats forever. Counts from the bad log: ReturnType(qsort) revised 32,356x; append activation facts retracted+republished 10,782x each.

Four root defects: (1) absence read as the type none; (2) last-write-wins returns permit descent; (3) waiting retracts the waiter's own claims; (4) contribution sets derived from mid-fixpoint reads retract sibling demand facts.

== THE DESIGN (three rules; no pass structure enshrined in the engine) ==
1. CLAIMS DECLARE THEIR SHAPE. FactKey gains is_cumulative(): true for ReturnType and ActivationInputs (their content is a monotone join; re-publication can only grow within an epoch), false for everything else (replacing: content overwrites). This is a content-algebra declaration (like choosing a CRDT type), NOT a pass ordering — it orders nothing.
2. WAITING EXTENDS, CONCLUDING REPLACES. A job completion with unmet waits unions its reads into its subscriptions, unions its outputs into its standing claims (retracting nothing), and marks all its claims dirty (a blocked publisher is never settled). Only a wait-free conclusion replaces reads and claims (retraction-by-omission remains available there, and is final).
3. ASCENTS WAKE, GROUND SHIFTS REBASE. Content growth of a cumulative fact = ascent: wake readers (re-run + join), exactly today. A content change to a replacing fact, any retraction, or a change published by a rebased job = ground shift: each reader gets its claims marked dirty, its job-level REBASE flag set, and is enqueued. A rebased job runs with replace-semantics on its cumulative stores (join is suspended for that conclude) and its changed keys propagate as shifts; if it re-derives equal values, changed=false and the cone stops. No claims are ever eagerly retracted by a shift — the shift cone is exactly the set of jobs whose recomputed outputs actually differ, preserving today's minimal-rerun incrementality (drive_test redefinition tests pin this).

Convergence then follows from chaotic-iteration/Kleene: cumulative claims make transfers monotone; the widening operator (child ticket) makes chains finite; any fair schedule reaches the same least fixpoint. Wake order becomes performance-only.

Two companion principles from the old-world engine (src/type_infer/mod.rs — module doc section "Unknown is not none"), adopted as DELETIONS, not as imported machinery: ABSENCE NEVER BECOMES A TYPE (the body walk gets Option-typed path results; the type none only ever arrives as a proven fact) and "ANY IS EARNED" (the solver never uses any() as a placeholder for not-yet-proven; any survives only at provider boundaries). The old-world 4-state Info cell, absorbing Unknown, global status enum, and ValueProof plane are explicitly NOT imported — compiler2's fact settledness carries completeness per-fact.

== ARC B (after Arc A) ==
Numeric literal types (int_lit/float_lit singleton types) leave the compiler2 lattice; atoms keep finite/cofinite singleton sets. Elixir's Module.Types.Descr is the model: integer()/float() are single bitmap bits; the literal 12 types as integer() even in a pattern; constant dispatch compares VALUES in the pattern matcher. fz's own old world agreed (type_infer typed Const::Int as int() with the literal as a ValueProof witness, never a type). Literal numerics in TYPE position widen to integer()/float() with a warning (user decision; Elixir-surface compatibility). The flow/observed split in semantic analysis exists only because numeric literals needed widening at merges — it dissolves entirely.

== ORDER AND VERIFICATION ==
Children execute in numeric order (dependencies enforce it). TDD per ticket. Before each commit: fresh uniquely-marked test runs; zero new failures vs baseline (KNOWN pre-existing failures, NOT yours to fix here: compiler2_native_program_jit_keeps_tail_recursion_bounded stack-overflows the test binary — skip it with -- --skip; plus 4 pre-existing failures tracked elsewhere: compiler2_native_program_joins_callable_resume_before_materializing_closure_call, case_joined_fn_ref_remains_callable, guard_and_binding_reuse_single_list_head_extraction, spec_unknown_type_is_resolve_error); zero warnings. End state: 20 consecutive quicksort builds with stable job counts and megabyte logs; the deepening-recursion fixture terminates by widening instead of timeout. After the epic: re-examine the fz-rh2.18.x tickets — several hand-manage freshness these rules now own structurally.

comments.text: Baseline accounting addition: ir_planner::quicksort_partition_accumulators_converge_to_one_spec and ir_planner::return_capabilities_classify_quicksort_fn_shapes fail at the fz-rh2.21.2 tip (old pipeline, untouched by this epic) — pre-existing alongside the known trio + fz-rh2.20 overflow. To be re-verified in the 21.9 closeout sweep.

EPIC COMPLETE — all nine children landed as nine commits (26845f05d..HEAD). Final accounting:

ARC A (convergence): the quicksort runaway is dead by construction. 20 release-binary builds: stable megabyte logs (was 2.5MB-or-700MB by hash seed). 20 in-process drives: identical 23-activation frontiers every schedule, ~310 jobs (was 54,000+). Max return ascents on the corpus: 3-4 (widening delay 8, never engaged outside the deepening fixture). A settled world re-drives with zero jobs. The lib suite COMPLETES in ~10s where the baseline run hung.

ARC B (literals): IntSet/FloatSet/F64Bits/widen_literal_flow/widen_for_recursive_spec_key deleted; the flow/observed split deleted (-174 lines in the analyzer alone); numeric literals in type position widen with type/numeric-literal-widened; constants ride the lowering as values (LoweredMapKey, dispatch consts). Two real latent bugs found and fixed by the rewrite: string-constant dispatch silently deleted live wildcard clauses (miss-side subtype proof on a singleton-less kind), and the runtime predicate read any basic bit as binary.

DELTA vs the fz-rh2.19.4 baseline: zero new failures, one baseline failure fixed (quicksort frontier canary), +5 passing; matrix delta exactly zero (48/517 both sides — that breakage and the ~525 lib failures pre-exist this epic, rooted at or before the fz-rh2.18.5 WIP state). Known out-of-scope: fz-rh2.20 (JIT stack overflow), the 4 known compiler2 failures, 2 pre-existing ir_planner quicksort failures (commented earlier).

title: compiler2 semantic fixpoint non-convergence: recursive activation return oscillates; schedule-order non-determinism decides settling
